Collage theartificialintelligens AI infrastructure may be entering a new era. This research introduces Prefill-as-a-Service (PrfaaS) — a system that splits AI inference across datacenters to make large models faster and cheaper to run. Instead of forcing one cluster to handle everything, remote high-compute GPUs process long prompts, while local clusters focus on token generation. The result? • 54% higher throughput • ~50% faster Time-To-First-Token for long prompts • Only 13% bandwidth usage on a 100 Gbps link The biggest insight: newer hybrid models shrink KVCache enough that cross-datacenter serving is now practical. That means future LLMs may run on distributed global infrastructure instead of one giant datacenter. This could reshape how frontier AI systems are deployed at scale. #AI #LLM #MachineLearning #Datacenter #ArtificialIntelligence

theartificialintelligens AI infrastructure may be entering a new era.

This research introduces Prefill-as-a-Service (PrfaaS) — a system that splits AI inference across datacenters to make large models faster and cheaper to run. Instead of forcing one cluster to handle everything, remote high-compute GPUs process long prompts, while local clusters focus on token generation.

The result?
• 54% higher throughput
• ~50% faster Time-To-First-Token for long prompts
• Only 13% bandwidth usage on a 100 Gbps link

The biggest insight: newer hybrid models shrink KVCache enough that cross-datacenter serving is now practical. That means future LLMs may run on distributed global infrastructure instead of one giant datacenter.

This could reshape how frontier AI systems are deployed at scale.

#AI #LLM #MachineLearning #Datacenter #ArtificialIntelligence
